data.title: Stocks jump, dollar higher in wake of US jobs numbers
data.description:
Key points:
  • Stocks gain after jobs data
  • December nonfarm payrolls rise less than forecast
  • Dollar initially pares gains after data
  • European stocks end at record high

By Caroline Valetkevitch

Stock indexes gained and the dollar was higher on Friday after data showed the U.S. economy created fewer jobs than expected in December, supporting the view the Federal Reserve would leave interest rates unchanged this month.

The S&P 500 hit an intraday high and the STOXX 600 ended at a record. Chip stocks rose and helped to boost the S&P 500, with Intel gaining after U.S. President Donald Trump said he had a "great meeting" with the chipmaker's Chief Executive Officer, Lip-Bu Tan.

The Bureau of Labor Statistics monthly report showed 50,000 workers were added to nonfarm payrolls in December, compared with expectations in a Reuters poll for a rise of 60,000, just above November's downwardly revised increase of 56,000. The unemployment rate eased, as expected, to 4.4%.

Fed funds futures traders are now pricing in only a 4.8% chance of a rate cut at the Fed's Jan. 27-28 meeting, down from 11.6% before the data. The next cut is unlikely before at least April.

"Payrolls were a little bit light relative to consensus, but still fairly strong numbers," said Tim Ghriskey, senior portfolio strategist at Ingalls & Snyder in New York.

"We are back to normal in terms of economic reporting so that's a bit of a relief for everyone."

Before now, the release of U.S. economic data had been delayed because of the long federal government shutdown.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 252.84 points, or 0.51%, to 49,518.95, the S&P 500 rose 53.23 points, or 0.77%, to 6,974.69 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 216.38 points, or 0.92%, to 23,696.40.

All three indexes are set for weekly gains in the first full trading week of 2026, with the Dow on track for its biggest weekly gain since the last week of November.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI rose 6.01 points, or 0.58%, to 1,035.46.

European shares ended at a record high. A jump in Glencore helped put the STOXX 600 on its longest weekly winning streak since May. The pan-European STOXX 600 index rose 0.97%.

DOLLAR, TWO-YEAR YIELDS UP

After the jobs report the dollar initially gave up almost all the day's gains versus a basket of major currencies , having risen by nearly 0.2% earlier. The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, was last up 0.26% at 99.13.

Interest rate sensitive two-year Treasury yields were higher after the data, while 10-year yields were last down slightly. The 2-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Fed, rose 5 basis points to 3.538%, from 3.488% late on Thursday. The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes fell 1.2 basis points to 4.171%.

In commodities, crude oil ended sharply higher. Brent futures rose $1.35, or 2.18%, to settle at $63.34 per barrel, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude gained $1.36, or 2.35%, to $59.12.

Investors have become more convinced that production in Venezuela, even under U.S. control, may not rise meaningfully for some time.

SUPREME COURT WON'T RULE ON TRUMP'S TARIFFS FRIDAY

Investors had been bracing for a possible U.S. Supreme Court ruling on the legality of Trump's tariffs.

But the court is expected to issue its next rulings on Jan. 14. The court indicated on its website on Friday that it could release decisions in argued cases when the justices take the bench during a scheduled sitting next Wednesday.

data.title: 2-Year Treasury Yield Rises to 3.539% This Week — Data Talk
data.description:
The 2-year yield rose 0.063 percentage point to 3.539% this week. The price fell 4/32 to 99 22/32.

  • Largest one-week yield gain since Friday, Dec. 5, 2025
  • Snaps a four-week streak of falling yields
  • Today it is up 0.052 percentage point and the price fell 3/32 price points
  • Largest one-day yield gain since Wednesday, Oct. 29, 2025
  • Yield is up for two consecutive trading days
  • Yield is up 0.071 percentage point over the last two trading days
  • Largest two-day yield gain since Friday, Dec. 5, 2025
  • Yield is up five of the past seven trading days
  • Highest yield since Wednesday, Dec. 10, 2025,
  • Yield is off 0.860 percentage point from its 52-week high of 4.399% hit Monday, Jan. 13, 2025
  • Yield is up 0.113 percentage point from its 52-week low of 3.426% hit Thursday, Oct. 16, 2025
  • Yield is down 0.855 percentage point from 52 weeks ago

  • Month-to-date the yield is up 0.071 percentage point
  • Year-to-date the yield is up 0.071 percentage point

Data based on 3 p.m. ET values

data.title: Exxon CEO says prepared to evaluate reentering Venezuela
data.description:
Exxon Mobil CEO Darren Woods said on Friday the U.S. oil major could hit the ground running in Venezuela and evaluate potentially returning to Venezuela.

"It's absolutely critical that we get a technical team in place to assess the current state of the industry," Woods said.

The comments were made during a White House meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday with other oil executives.

data.title: Forgent Power Solutions, Electrical Distribution Equipment Manufacturer, Files for NYSE IPO
data.description:
Forgent Power Solutions filed for an initial public offering to list on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker "FPS." The Minnesota-based company designs and manufactures electrical distribution equipment used in data centers, the power grid and industrial facilities. Proceeds will be used to purchase interests in its operating subsidiary in a tax-efficient Up-C structure; selling stockholders will not receive proceeds.

Business Description

Forgent Power Solutions designs and manufactures electrical distribution equipment that safely and efficiently delivers power across data centers, the grid and energy‑intensive industrial facilities. Its portfolio spans automatic transfer switches, medium- and low-voltage switchgear and switchboards, dry-type and liquid-filled transformers, PDUs, remote power panels, generator connection cabinets, tap boxes, power skids and eHouses. The company focuses heavily on custom, engineered‑to‑order products and integrated Powertrain Solutions for technically demanding applications.

Forgent sells to technology companies, utilities, independent power producers, industrial operators, EPCs, integrators, OEMs and electrical distributors. It differentiates through short lead times, broad in‑house capabilities (including transformer manufacturing), deep engineering resources, and the ability to deliver prefabricated systems that shift labor from the field to the factory. Manufacturing spans campuses in Minnesota, Texas, Maryland, California and Mexico, with an ongoing capacity expansion designed to support significant growth and large-scale projects for data centers and grid infrastructure.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: U.S. electrical distribution equipment market was about $33.1 billion in 2025 (BCE).
  • Market growth: BCE forecasts ~20% CAGR from 2025 to 2030, driven by data centers, grid expansion, and manufacturing reshoring.
  • Market position: Company is a leading U.S. designer and manufacturer with breadth across medium/low-voltage gear, transformers, PDUs, eHouses and prefabricated solutions.
  • Key competitors: Vertiv, Schneider Electric, Hubbell/PCX, IEM, Hitachi Energy, GE-Prolec, Eaton (Cooper), nVent, WEG, Systems Control.
  • Industry trends: Rapid data center buildouts (AI), accelerating grid load growth, expansion of prefabricated solutions, and faster-growing demand for custom engineered products.

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: Fiscal 2025 revenue mix—Data Center 42%, Grid 23%, Industrial 19%, Other 16%
  • Locations: Ten manufacturing campuses totaling ~2.3 million sq. ft. across MN, TX, MD, CA and Mexico
  • Geographic presence: Substantially all revenue from North American customers
  • Partnerships: Multi‑channel go‑to‑market with direct sales and application engineers, third‑party sales representatives, integrators and OEM private‑label relationships
  • Orders/GMV: Backlog of $1,027.1 million as of September 30, 2025
  • Other key metrics: >1,500 unique designs annually; average batch count ~15; >200 new customers added in fiscal 2025; 37% of Sep. 30, 2025 backlog from new customers; Powertrain Solutions grew from 4% to 13% of revenue (FY24 to FY25); service revenue ~4% of FY25; capacity expansion plan ~$205 million, 1.8 million sq. ft., designed to support up to ~$5 billion annual revenue

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): $753.2 million (fiscal 2025)
  • Revenue growth: 56% year over year (fiscal 2025 vs. fiscal 2024 pro forma)
  • Gross profit: $278.1 million
  • Operating income: $72.2 million
  • Net income: $17.4 million

Management

  • Gary J. Niederpruem, Chief Executive Officer and Director - Former CBO/CEO at Cenergistic; senior roles at Vertiv and Emerson Network Power; MBA, University of Notre Dame.
  • Ryan S. Fiedler, Chief Financial Officer - Former CFO of Caterpillar’s Resource Industries segment; prior roles in investor relations and strategy at Caterpillar; BBA, Baylor University.
  • Tyson K. Hottinger, Chief Legal Officer - Former CLO of Array Technologies; prior private practice leadership; JD, University of Utah.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: Forgent Power Solutions, Inc.
  • Filing date: January 09, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: FPS
  • Exchange: NYSE
  • Lead underwriters: Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC; Jefferies; Morgan Stanley; J.P. Morgan; BofA Securities; Barclays; TD Cowen; MUFG; Wolfe | Nomura Alliance; KeyBanc Capital Markets; Oppenheimer & Co.
  • Use of proceeds: Use net proceeds to indirectly purchase Opco LLC Interests from Opco; Opco will redeem interests from existing owners. No proceeds to selling stockholders.

Original SEC Filing: Forgent Power Solutions, Inc. - S-1 - Jan. 09, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

(1) Report will include initial estimates for the prior month period. A separate report for the prior month period will not be released.

(2) BLS will not produce an October 2025 Producer Price Index (PPI) news release. BLS is collecting October reference period data on a delay due to the lapse in appropriations. BLS plans to publish October data with the November 2025 PPI release.

(3) BLS will not produce an October 2025 Import and Export Price Indexes news release. BLS could not completely collect October 2025 reference period survey data due to a lapse in appropriations. BLS is unable to retroactively collect these data. For many indexes, BLS uses nonsurvey data sources instead of or in addition to survey data to make the index calculations. BLS is able to retroactively acquire most of the nonsurvey data for October. Where possible, BLS will publish October 2025 values for these series with the release of November 2025 data. BLS plans to publish October data with the November 2025 release.

(4) The January 22 GDP Q3 release replaces the typical third estimate. A second estimate of Q3 Corporate Profits will be included in the release.

data.title: ConocoPhillips says US banks may need to back Venezuela oil investments
data.description: ConocoPhillips CEO Ryan Lance says U.S. banks including the Export‑Import Bank may need to be involved in financing Venezuela oil investments.

data.title: Fed's Barkin Doesn't Expect Low-Hire Low-Fire Environment To Last — Market Talk
data.description: Richmond Fed President Tom Barkin says the "low hire, low fire" job picture doesn't feel like one that's going to persist, in an interview with Kyla Scanlon. In 2026 - Barkin says he's watching the hiring and the layoff market very closely. When discussing this outlook, Barkin questions whether the market will break more toward hiring, or more towards firing. "If demand stays as strong as it is, eventually people are going to need to hire folks to fulfill that demand," he says. When discussing AI, he says the scenario could be more positive for young people if companies lean on AI adoption.(jessica.coacci@wsj.com; @jessica_coacci)

data.title: Venu Holding Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
Venu Holding expanded its strategic relationship with Aramark through two material agreements. First, the parties amended their binding letter of intent to add exclusive food, beverage, retail, custodial, grounds, and facility services for planned amphitheaters in El Paso and the greater Houston area, with a 10-year term tied to the earliest opening of the Broken Arrow or McKinney venues. Second, Aramark committed an additional $10.005 million investment in Venu via a staged private placement of Series B 4% Cumulative Convertible Preferred Stock, with confirmed registration rights subject to Rule 144. These agreements are designed to standardize operations across venues and provide capital for development.

Agreement 1: Venu Holding Signs LOI Amendment With Aramark

  • Agreement type: First Amendment to Binding Letter of Intent for exclusive venue services
  • Counterparty: Aramark
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 05 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 10 years from earliest opening of Broken Arrow or McKinney amphitheaters
  • Reason: Expand exclusive services across additional venues

Agreement 2: Venu Holding Signs Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement With Aramark

  • Agreement type: Private placement of Series B 4% Cumulative Convertible Preferred Stock with registration rights
  • Counterparty: Aramark
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 06 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Fund venue development and align strategic partner

Original SEC Filing: Venu Holding Corp [ VENU ] - 8-K - Jan. 09, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: It's the End of the World as We Know It — and the Market Feels Fine — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Avi Salzman

Americans woke up to a world remade in the first full trading week of 2026. For investors, it's a change they can believe in.

The biggest news was President Donald Trump's decision to "run" Venezuela after capturing its president, Nicolás Maduro. But the stock market is being remade too, as investors turn to new kinds of assets and Trump agitates for change in areas as diverse as defense, energy, and housing. Even market leadership seems to be changing, with investors tilting to value stocks and small companies.

Change could be worrisome after a year when Big Tech companies propelled the S&P 500 to a 16% gain. But so far, no one seems to mind. The index is on track for a new record, up 1.6% on the week and trading within sneezing distance of 7000. The Dow Jones Industrial Average is set to gain 2.4% and also could touch a new record, and the Nasdaq Composite is on track to rise 1.9%, though the S&P 500 information technology sector is up just 0.2%. It seems tech can take a breather and markets can still gain.

So far this year, areas like materials and industrials are far outpacing tech names. And small-caps are doing the best of all, with the Russell 2000 index already up around 6%.

"I think the broadening out of the market into other sectors should give investors optimism that it's an increasingly diversified market," says Eric Teal, chief investment officer at Comerica Wealth Management. "We're getting small-cap outperformance, and even microcap outperformance, which we pay close attention to."

The market is strong enough now to overlook disappointing economic news. Investors shrugged off weaker-than-expected December job gains on Friday. The economy added 50,000 jobs, though the unemployment rate is still just 4.4%.

Even as small-caps rise, some former winners in tech have been lagging. As of Friday afternoon, Apple stock was on track for its first gain in eight trading sessions. And even on its best day of the year, it was up less than 1%.

Is this the long-awaited bursting of the artificial-intelligence bubble? Early signs say no. In fact, Teal thinks that the tech stocks need the rest of the market to prove that AI doesn't just benefit the big guys. "The adoption of AI has to be broad-based," he said.

Traders and strategists cautioned investors not to overreact to these early shifts. Some are seasonal and incremental. And frankly, the move is marginal compared with longer-term trends. The market has been heavily unbalanced for years toward Big Tech names, so any rebalancing is welcome.

"It is impressive to see these unloved indices get a few days in the sun, " writes Michael O'Rourke, chief market strategist at JonesTrading. "Nonetheless, the moves are essentially blips compared to the massive valuation gap that has developed over the past five years." He points out that the S&P 500's price/earnings ratio is 49% higher than that of the S&P Small Cap 600 index.

The Venezuela raid shows the world is changing fast. It turns out the market can change just as quickly.

Write to Avi Salzman at avi.salzman@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Lumber Rebounds From September Lows
data.description:
Lumber futures rose toward $535 per thousand board feet, rebounding from the September low of $528 reached on January 7th after a low liquidity holiday sell off unwound, improving seasonal demand expectations and longer term supply tightening.

Renewed engagement from market participants, signaled that forced selling and the thin trading conditions that pushed prices to multi month lows have faded.

Seasonal demand expectations have strengthened as builders begin positioning ahead of the spring construction period, when consumption typically improves following year end destocking.

Industry forecasts point to a modest pickup in US housing starts and repair and remodel activity in 2026 as interest rates ease and trade uncertainty recedes, supporting demand after a weak finish to 2025.

At the same time, longer term supply growth remains constrained by ongoing tariffs on Canadian softwood and slower capacity expansion across North American sawmills, limiting surplus.

data.title: Trump says China can buy all oil it wants from US or Venezuela
data.description:
President Donald Trump said on Friday China can buy all the oil it wants from either the United States or Venezuela in the wake of the U.S. arrest of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro.

In remarks with oil executives at the White House, Trump said China or Russia might have moved on Venezuela's oil if the United States had not intervened to oust Maduro.

data.title: Exxon CEO says the challenge in Venezuela is developing the resources
data.description:
Exxon CEO Darren Woods said on Friday the challenge in Venezuela is developing the oil resources.

The comments were made during a White House meeting on Friday.

data.title: CBOT soybeans end up as traders adjust positions before USDA data
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures closed slightly higher on Friday due to traders adjusting positions in the grain markets before the U.S. government releases highly anticipated U.S. crop reports next week, analysts said.

  • Analysts on average expect the U.S. Department of Agriculture will slightly lower its estimates for the country's soybean production and yields in a report due out on Monday, according to a Reuters poll.

  • The agency will also issue quarterly data on U.S. grain and soy stocks as of December 1.

  • Some traders were unwinding long corn and short soybean positions ahead of the reports, a broker said.

  • Exporters sold 198,000 metric tons of U.S. soybeans to unknown destinations, the USDA said in a daily reporting system, after it reported sales of 132,000 tons of U.S. soybeans to China on Thursday.

  • China's Sinograin will auction 1.1 million metric tons of imported soybeans on January 13, its first sale of the year and the fourth since last month, as the state stockpiler works to make room for arriving U.S. shipments.

  • CBOT March soybeans (SH26) closed up 1-1/4 cents at $10.62-1/2 a bushel. For the week, the contract rose 1.6%.

  • CBOT March soymeal (SMH26) edged up $0.10 to end at $303.70 per short ton, and CBOT March soyoil (BOH26) rose 0.24 cent to close at 49.69 cents per pound.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Falls on Loose Supply Outlook — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas ends the day and the week lower amid market uncertainty that an expected cooldown in late January weather will keep the supply surplus from growing. "The market seems to be significantly discounting any balance-of-winter risk and the forward curve is reflective of that idea," says Andy Huenefeld of Pinebrook Energy Advisors. Record LNG exports which would normally hold prices up are easily absorbed by lower demand and record production seen so far in 2026, he says. With no extreme weather on the horizon, "the path of least resistance looks like continuing to the downside." Nymex gas for February delivery falls 7% to $3.169/mmBtu,down 12% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Corrects Lower to End Week
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market closed lower Friday, seeing a modest correction to end the week after hitting fresh one-month highs in early activity.

* The March contract climbed above nearby resistance Friday morning to hit a session high of C$638.10 per tonne.

However, the buying interest subsided, and the contract was well below that level at the close.

* Gains in Chicago soybeans and soyoil provided spillover support, with European rapeseed also higher on the day.

* Optimism over Prime Minister Mark Carney's visit China next week remained a feature, with ideas the trip could lead to an easing of Chinese tariffs on Canadian canola.

* Canada exported 147,800 tonnes of canola during the week ended Jan. 4, which was up from 121,000 tonnes the previous week. Crop-year-to-date exports of 2.8 million tonnes remain well short of the 4.7 million tonnes exported through 22 weeks of the previous marketing year.

* There were 69,248 contracts traded on Thursday, which compares with Thursday when 39,246 contracts changed hands.

Spreading accounted for 32,556 of the contracts traded.

Settlement prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne.

 
           Price      Change

May       633.00     dn 3.40 
Jul       640.00     dn 3.80 
Nov       637.10     dn 3.60

data.title: GE Vernova Seen With Rising Concern of Overcapacity — Market Talk
data.description: GE Vernova faces rising concerns from investors regarding power capacity and a potential oversupply in the market, Baird analysts say in a note. The worries come after several competitors announced plans to enter the power space, including the likes of Doosan, FTAI Aviation and Caterpillar, the analysts say. As new capacity comes online, GE Vernova's pricing power for new bookings will likely come under pressure, they say. Such concerns could dominate the narrative around the stock in the near term, though the company will likely still be able to meet or exceed estimates through 2028, they say. "Our long-term outlook for GEV as a leader in both power and electrification and viewing GEV as a core holding are unchanged, but we are moving to the sidelines near term," they say, downgrading the stock to neutral from outperform.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)
